content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

Модель данных в одной строке` defines the compact roles of the main memory entities: `Source` records what was said or observed, `Entity` identifies who or what something is, `Note` stores important useful context not yet cemented as fact, `Claim` stores normalized facts, `Profile` is a compact projection of stable context, `Task` records...[truncated 118 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, summarizing the one-line purpose of each v3 memo...[truncated 21 chars] entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1>,entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<7> keywords=Source,Entity,Note,Claim,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun,one-line model,agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md tags=doc_digest,data_model,memory_entities evidence=## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory data model layered architecture summary=The `v3` memory data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by separating memory into layers: `Source` as evidence, `Entity`/`Claim` as graph-shaped semantics, `Note` as rich intermediate context, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode` as operational projections, and `MemoryRun` as audit. scope=Document section `02. Общие cross-cutting поля` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing the overall v3 memory da...[truncated 22 chars] keywords=v3,memory data model,Source,Entity,Claim,Note,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun,layers tags=data_model,architecture,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note as intermediate semantic memory fragment summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment for important, retrieval-useful context that should not yet be cemented as a hard fact; typical uses include design direction, decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining Note purpose and use cases. keywords=Note,semantic memory fragment,retrieval,design direction,decision rationale,hypotheses,doc digests tags=doc_digest,data_model,note useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Entity as canonical memory node with aliases and reversible merges summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Entity` is the canonical memory node used to unify different mentions of the same object and anchor claims, tasks, notes, and episodes. It supports aliases and merge while preserving history: aliases must point to the current canonical entity, and mistaken merges must be reversible through an audit trail. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the Entity component role, aliases, ...[truncated 31 chars] keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,aliases,merge,audit trail,claims,tasks,notes,episodes tags=data_model,entity,aliases,merge,invariants useCount=0

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
